author: Austin Crawford
title: Harbor Ghost Tour
summary: Block Island Ghost Tours' New Harbor Walking Tour, has been renamed to the New Harbor Ghost Tour. Our Ghost Host (tour guide) will lead you on a walk around the New Harbor area. We learned a lot about some of the most devastating shipwrecks and less welcome visitors. They physically left long ago, but what of them remains? Did you know a group of real-life vampires may have used the island as a meeting place? The history of Block Island has always included those who travel by water. We highly recommend this tour to all of our Island boater's and anyone else looking to hear some of Block Island's haunted history.
content:
Block Island Ghost Tours' New Harbor Walking Tour, has been renamed to the New Harbor Ghost Tour. Our Ghost Host (tour guide) will lead you on a walk around the New Harbor area. We learned a lot about some of the most devastating shipwrecks and less welcome visitors. They physically left long ago, but what of them remains? Did you know a group of real-life vampires may have used the island as a meeting place? The history of Block Island has always included those who travel by water. We highly recommend this tour to all of our Island boater's and anyone else looking to hear some of Block Island's haunted history.

Every tour includes a free flashlight, an “I Survived” sticker, and a $5 discount on any of our t-shirts.

 

Call 401-477-2979 or email blockislandghosttours@gmail.com to book private group tours.

Location: Check-in in front of Dead Eyed Dick’s Restaurant 

Price: $35/person. Children under 12 are $25

Time: Wednesday, Thursday & Friday's at 7pm         

Length: 60-75 minutes

Distance: 1 mile

 

Click the link below to book a tour.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Ecumenical Choir Is Back
summary: The Block Island Ecumenical Choir returns after two years and will be rehearsing for the upcoming season, which includes Christmas and Easter concerts. Rehearsals are on Tuesday nights from 7 to 9 p.m. starting Oct. 11 at the Harbor Church. All ages and abilities are welcome as long as you can carry a tune. Hope to see you there.
content: The Block Island Ecumenical Choir returns after two years and will be rehearsing for the upcoming season, which includes Christmas and Easter concerts. Rehearsals are on Tuesday nights from 7 to 9 p.m. starting Oct. 11 at the Harbor Church. All ages and abilities are welcome as long as you can carry a tune. Hope to see you there.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Cemetery Tour
summary: "Stories in Stone under the Moonlight"
content:
"Stories in Stone under the Moonlight"

Karin Sprague, Stone Carver and Pam Gasner, Executive Director of the Historical Society lead you on a guided tour through the oldest section of the Island Cemetery.

Please wear comfortable walking shoes and bring a flashlight.  Meet at the north entrance near the skateboard park. 

$15 for Adults, $10 for members and $5 for kids.  There will be door prizes given away.

Taking place rain or shine.
address: New Shoreham New Shoreham Washington County

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Bingo at the Fire Barn
summary: Bingo? Popular? Believe it. On Block Island, it’s the place to see and be seen, to rub elbows with islanders and visitors alike, and to catch up with local news and views. And the game itself? Buy a few cards and see if you can beat everyone to be first to call BINGO. It’s another simple summer pleasure, Block Island-style.
content:
Bingo? Popular? Believe it. On Block Island, it’s the place to see and be seen, to rub elbows with islanders and visitors alike, and to catch up with local news and views. And the game itself? Buy a few cards and see if you can beat everyone to be first to call BINGO. It’s another simple summer pleasure, Block Island-style. 

Block Island Bingo is more than fun and games, it’s also an important fundraiser for the Fire Department.

Each year, the BIVFD Auxiliary raises about $3000 from this Tuesday night tradition. Doors open at 6:30 pm, the first card is called at 7 pm,

Admission: Just $5 per person.

meta.description: Bingo every Tuesday night on Block Island at the Firebarn.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Night Sky Viewing at Hodge Preserve
summary: Local naturalist narrates the season’s constellations, planets, and maybe a shooting star. Bring a blanket or beach chair, flashlight, and insect repellant for your viewing comfort. If the night sky is obscured by clouds, fog, or rain the program will be postponed to the following night.
content:
Local naturalist narrates the season’s constellations, planets, and maybe a shooting star. Bring a blanket or beach chair, flashlight, and insect repellant for your viewing comfort. If the night sky is obscured by clouds, fog, or rain the program will be postponed to the following night.

Advance sign-up is recommended but not required, email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Aging Disgracefully Comedy Revue at Yellow Kittens
summary: Charlie Hall and Doreen Collins have created a new hit show called AGING DISGRACEFULLY. It's a mix of stand-up, song, satire and skits on the perks and  pitfalls of getting old. From botox to bifocals to bingo, this dynamic duo- both members of the RI Comedy Hall of Fame- covers it all in this very interactive 75 min comedy cabaret.
content:
Charlie Hall and Doreen Collins have created a new hit show called AGING DISGRACEFULLY. It's a mix of stand-up, song, satire and skits on the perks and  pitfalls of getting old. From botox to bifocals to bingo, this dynamic duo- both members of the RI Comedy Hall of Fame- covers it all in this very interactive 75 min comedy cabaret.

Get Tickets Here!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Earth Day - Night Walk - James Stover Exploration
summary: Join us for a night walk and hopefully glimpse the Lyrid meteor shower at its peak. Meet at Hodge Preserve parking area.
content:
Join us for a night walk and hopefully glimpse the Lyrid meteor shower at its peak. Meet at Hodge Preserve parking area.

 

Advance sign-up is encouraged but not required. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org. 

 

 

The James Stover Block Island Exploration Series

Discover the fascinating nature of Block Island. Guided monthly walks for families (with kids of all ages) and individuals. Explore areas and species special to Block Island.  These walks are funded by friends of James Stover (1996-2003), in memory of a boy who loved exploring Block Island.

 

Programs are weather dependent

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Thursday Night at the Museum at the BI Historical Society
summary: "Block Island Days" featuring the recorded music of bluegrass singer Rob Raroux and friends. Come listen to some great Island themed tunes while watching what happens here beyond tourism. Shop on the lawn begins at 7:30 at the Block Island Historical Society.  Music and film presentation starts at sunset. CDs will be available for purchase. FREE/ MASKS REQUIRED/ WEATHER PERMITTING

author: Robert Lucier
title: (Thursday) Night at the Museum: Presented by the BI Historical Society.
summary: Kicks off at 7:30 pm with a sidewalk sale at the corner of Old Town Rd. and Ocean ave.
content:
Kicks off at 7:30 pm with a sidewalk sale at the corner of Old Town Rd. and Ocean ave. 

Followed at 8:30 with a theater under the stars featuring "Beacon on the Bluff... the Rescue of the Southeast Lighthouse." 45 minute run time. Free.

Donations graciously accepted. MASKS REQUIRED. Weather dependent. 401-466-5758

author: Austin Crawford
title: Soundwaves Movies on the Beach Vicky Cristina Barcelona
summary: Soundwaves presents Vicky Cristina Barcelona rated PG-13
content:
Soundwaves presents Vicky Cristina Barcelona rated PG-13

author: Austin Crawford
title: Food Safety Classes
summary: FOOD SAFETY CERTIFICATION/RECERTIFICATION CLASSES FOR 2024
content:
FOOD SAFETY CERTIFICATION/RECERTIFICATION CLASSES FOR 2024

 

CERTIFICATION/ CLASSES --- Registration fee of $205 includes textbook, study guide and examination for this 8 Hour Course. Textbook will be mailed to you prior to class.

 

 

 

RECERTIFICATION CLASSES --- New regulation requires everyone being recertified to take the exam. You have 3 choices:

 

  1. Exam only:  $95.00
  2. Review class, study guide and exam, no  textbook:  $170.00
  3. Textbook, study guide, review class and exam:  $205.00.  Textbook will be mailed to you prior to class.

Includes exam

 

Click the link to book now and pick June 10th.

https://www.foodsafety-certification.com/services
